title: Plivo Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Plivo** is a cloud communications platform exposing a REST API for voice + messaging at `https://api.plivo.com/v1/Account/{auth_id}`. This connector — `PlivoConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "plivo"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Plivo account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Account | `/Account/{auth_id}/` | Fetch account record (used as health probe) |
| Messages | `/Account/{auth_id}/Message/` | Send/list SMS + MMS, fetch by UUID |
| Calls | `/Account/{auth_id}/Call/` | Initiate, list, fetch, hangup, transfer calls |
| Recordings | `/Account/{auth_id}/Recording/` | List + fetch call recordings |
| Numbers | `/Account/{auth_id}/Number/` + `/PhoneNumber/` | List owned numbers, search marketplace, buy, fetch |
| Applications | `/Account/{auth_id}/Application/` | List + create + fetch voice applications (webhook configs) |
| Pricing | `/Account/{auth_id}/Pricing/` | Per-country voice + SMS rates |
| Subaccounts | `/Account/{auth_id}/Subaccount/` | List subaccounts under the master |
| Endpoints | `/Account/{auth_id}/Endpoint/` | List SIP endpoints |

The connector normalises messages + calls into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::PlivoHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Obtain Plivo credentials

1. Sign in to the [Plivo Console](https://console.plivo.com/).
2. Open **Account → Account Settings → Account**.
3. Copy the **Auth ID** (looks like `MAxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx`).
4. Copy the **Auth Token** (treat as a password — never share or commit).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Plivo**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `auth_id` (text) | Plivo Account Auth ID |
| `auth_token` (secret) | Plivo Account Auth Token |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.plivo.com/v1` for regional endpoints |
| `default_caller_id` (text, optional) | Default sender number for `send_sms` / `make_call` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Client-side soft cap, default 60 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `auth_id` + `auth_token` are present (it does NOT call the API), initialises the `PlivoHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which fetches `GET /Account/{auth_id}/` as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + FAILED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Plivo REST API uses **HTTP Basic authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `auth_id` and `auth_token` are concatenated as `auth_id:auth_token`, base64-encoded, and sent as `Authorization: Basic <b64>` on every request.
- The `auth_id` is also embedded as a path segment in every account-scoped URL: `/v1/Account/{auth_id}/...`.
- `PlivoHTTPClient._basic_auth_header()` rebuilds the header on every request — there is no persistent session state.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `auth_id` + `auth_token`. Initialises `PlivoHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Plivo endpoint. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a synthetic `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured `auth_token` (for interface compatibility — there is no token exchange). |
| `health_check()` | `GET /Account/{auth_id}/` — minimal probe. |
| `on_token_refresh()` | No-op — Basic-auth credentials never expire. |
| token storage | None. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Basic base64(auth_id:auth_token)
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `auth_id` | text | yes | `self.auth_id` | Basic-auth username + URL path segment |
| `auth_token` | secret | yes | `self.auth_token` | Basic-auth password |
| `base_url` | text | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.plivo.com/v1`) | Override for Plivo regional endpoints / sandbox / proxy |
| `default_caller_id` | text | no | `self.default_caller_id` | Default sender number for outbound SMS / calls |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 60) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["auth_id", "auth_token"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "FAILED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    404: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://api.plivo.com/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `PlivoConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::PlivoHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `auth_id` + `auth_token`; initialise `PlivoHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the configured `auth_token`.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Fetches the account record via `GET /Account/{auth_id}/`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Pages recent Plivo messages + calls → `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`) → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Messaging (SMS / MMS)
children.content:
### `async send_sms(src, dst, text, type="sms", url=None, method="POST", log=True, trackable=False, message_uuid=None) -> Dict`
`POST /Message/`. Mirrors Plivo's documented body — pass `type="mms"` (or use `send_mms`) for media.

### `async send_mms(src, dst, text, url=None, ...) -> Dict`
Thin wrapper that forces `type="mms"`.

### `async get_message(message_uuid) -> Dict`
`GET /Message/{message_uuid}/`.

### `async list_messages(message_state=None, message_direction=None, from_=None, to=None, message_time__gte=None, message_time__lte=None, limit=20, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /Message/?…` — supports state/direction/sender/recipient/date-range filters.

children.title: Voice (Calls)
children.content:
### `async make_call(from_, to, answer_url, answer_method="POST", hangup_url=None, time_limit=14400, machine_detection="false", machine_detection_url=None) -> Dict`
`POST /Call/`. Plivo fetches XML instructions from `answer_url` when the callee answers.

### `async get_call(call_uuid) -> Dict`
`GET /Call/{call_uuid}/`.

### `async list_calls(subaccount=None, call_direction=None, from_=None, to=None, end_time__gte=None, end_time__lte=None, limit=20, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /Call/?…`.

### `async hangup_call(call_uuid) -> Dict`
`DELETE /Call/{call_uuid}/`. Plivo returns 204 — surfaced as `{}`.

### `async transfer_call(call_uuid, legs="aleg", aleg_url=None, aleg_method="POST", bleg_url=None) -> Dict`
`POST /Call/{call_uuid}/`.

children.title: Recordings
children.content:
### `async list_recordings(call_uuid=None, from_number=None, to_number=None, add_time__gte=None, add_time__lte=None, limit=20, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /Recording/?…`.

### `async get_recording(recording_id) -> Dict`
`GET /Recording/{recording_id}/`.

children.title: Numbers
children.content:
### `async list_numbers(type=None, number_startswith=None, subaccount=None, limit=20, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /Number/?…` — numbers attached to the account.

### `async get_number(number) -> Dict`
`GET /Number/{number}/`.

### `async search_phone_numbers(country_iso, type="local", pattern=None, region=None, services=None, limit=20, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /PhoneNumber/?country_iso=…` — search the Plivo marketplace. NOTE: this endpoint hits `/v1/PhoneNumber/` at the root, not under `/Account/{auth_id}/`.

### `async buy_phone_number(number) -> Dict`
`POST /PhoneNumber/{number}/`.

children.title: Applications
children.content:
### `async list_applications(subaccount=None, limit=20, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /Application/?…`.

### `async get_application(app_id) -> Dict`
`GET /Application/{app_id}/`.

### `async create_application(app_name, answer_url, answer_method="POST", hangup_url=None, message_url=None, message_method="POST") -> Dict`
`POST /Application/`.

children.title: Pricing · Subaccounts · Endpoints
children.content:
### `async get_pricing(country_iso) -> Dict`
`GET /Pricing/?country_iso=…` — per-country voice + SMS rates.

### `async list_subaccounts(limit=20, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /Subaccount/`.

### `async list_endpoints(limit=20, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /Endpoint/` — SIP endpoints under the account.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
PlivoError                            # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── PlivoAuthError                    # 401
├── PlivoNotFound                     # 404
├── PlivoRateLimitError               # 429
└── PlivoNetworkError                 # transport failure / 5xx
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 4xx | Raise immediately (typed exception) |
| 429 / 500 / 502 / 503 / 504 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE_S * 2 ** attempt + jitter` capped at `_BACKOFF_MAX_S=8.0`, retry up to `_max_retries=3` |
| `httpx.HTTPError` (timeout / connection refused / DNS) | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `PlivoNetworkError` |

A second tier of retry lives in `helpers/utils.py::with_retry()` — every connector public method wraps the HTTP call in `with_retry(..., max_retries=3)` so transient `PlivoRateLimitError` / `PlivoNetworkError` that escape the client also get a second chance.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches typed exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401  → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  FAILED)               — "Invalid Plivo credentials"
403  → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)  — sub-account forbidden
429  → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)            — logged as warning
network → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)           — Plivo unreachable
other → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `auth_id` or `auth_token` blank | Re-enter both in ACP. Plivo Console → Account → Account Settings shows them — rotate the token if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, FAILED)` at health_check | 401 from Plivo — bad `auth_id` / `auth_token` combination | Verify the Auth ID matches the account that minted the token. Rotate the token if you suspect compromise. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — subaccount disabled or the auth_id has been quarantined | Open a ticket with Plivo support and re-enable the subaccount. |
| `plivo.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Plivo per-account rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min`, batch fewer messages, or move to a higher Plivo plan. |
| `send_sms` returns 202 but no SMS arrives | `src` is not a Plivo number on this account, or destination country is restricted | Verify `src` is in the account's `list_numbers()` and that the destination country is enabled (Console → Messaging → Permissions). |
| `make_call` returns 201 but call never rings | `answer_url` is not reachable from Plivo's edge, or returns non-XML | Test the URL with `curl -X POST <answer_url>` from a public host. Plivo expects HTTP 200 + valid PlivoXML. |
| `buy_phone_number` 422 | Number not available, or KYC documents missing for that country | Re-run `search_phone_numbers(country_iso=…)` to confirm availability, then upload KYC docs in the Plivo Console. |
| 4xx with `message_uuid` containing a list | Plivo returns `message_uuid` as a 1-element list for `POST /Message/` — by design, not an error | The connector returns the raw body. Index `[0]` if you need the scalar. |
